videos.title: Why Bitcoin Could Hit New Highs in 2026
videos.text: Hey everyone, welcome back to the channel. Today we're diving deep into a question that is on every crypto investor's mind. Could Bitcoin hit new all-time highs by 2026? It's a bold prediction, but there are some compelling reasons to be optimistic. Let's break it down. First up, the Bitcoin hing. This is a built-in feature of Bitcoin's code that happens roughly every 4 years. And the next one is expected in 2024. The hing cuts the reward for mining new blocks in half. This reduces the new supply of Bitcoin entering the market. Historically, the year or two following a hing has seen significant price increases. If history rhymes, the period from 2024 to 2026 could be incredibly bullish. It's a classic supply and demand scenario. As new supply tightens, existing demand can push the price higher. Next, [music] let's talk about institutional adoption. We're not just talking about a few tech companies buying Bitcoin anymore. We're seeing major financial players like Black Rockck, Fidelity, and other Wall Street giants getting seriously involved. Their applications for Bitcoin spot ETFs, if approved, would be a gamecher. An ETF makes it incredibly easy for everyday investors and large institutions to get exposure to Bitcoin through their regular brokerage accounts. This could unlock a massive wave of new capital flowing into the market, potentially driving the price to levels we've never seen before. By 2026, these products could be well established and an integral part of the financial landscape. Another huge factor is the macroeconomic environment. Think about what's been happening with inflation and interest rates. Central banks around the world have been hiking rates to cool down inflation. But what happens when they need to pivot? If we see a shift back towards lower interest rates and more accommodative monetary policy, assets like Bitcoin, which are often seen as a hedge against currency debasement, could become extremely attractive. By 2026, the global economic cycle might be in a completely different phase, one that is much more favorable for scarce assets like Bitcoin. Then there's the Lightning Network. One of the biggest criticisms of Bitcoin has been its scalability. It can be slow and expensive for small everyday transactions. The Lightning Network is a second layer solution that's changing this, enabling instant and nearly free transactions. As this technology matures and gets more widely adopted over the next couple of years, Bitcoin's utility as a medium of exchange will skyrocket. By 2026, you might be buying your coffee with Bitcoin without even thinking about it. This increased utility adds fundamental value beyond just being a store of value. Finally, we have global adoption trends. We're seeing more countries and populations, especially in regions with unstable currencies turning to Bitcoin as a reliable store of value and a way to transact freely. El Salvador was just the beginning. As internet access expands and financial literacy grows, the network effect of Bitcoin will only get stronger. Every new user and every new business that accepts Bitcoin adds to its value. Over the next few years, this grassroots adoption could create a powerful and sustained tailwind for the price. So [music] when you combine the supply shock from the hing, the massive potential influx of institutional money from ETFs, [music] a potentially favorable macro backdrop, growing technological utility with the lightning network, and increasing global adoption, the case for a new all-time high by 2026 looks pretty strong. Of course, nothing is guaranteed in crypto, and it's essential to do your own research. This is not financial advice, but the confluence of these factors makes the next few years incredibly exciting for Bitcoin. What do you think? Will we see a new high by 2026? Let me know your thoughts in the comments below. Thanks for [music] watching and don't forget to like and subscribe for more crypto insights. See you in the next one.

videos.title: Why I'm Buying Bitcoin Here
videos.text: Bitcoin just fell about 50%. Everyone's asking if it's over. I'm doing the opposite, and here's why with the charts. This isn't financial advice. It's my thesis. So, let me actually show you the data. Bitcoin topped near $126,000 last October. Today, it's around 60, down 50%. The headlines are pure fear, but fear is exactly when I start paying attention. So, chart one. This is the 200-week moving average. In 2015, 2018, and 2022, it caught the exact bottom. Right now, price is sitting directly on that line again. Historically, that's where accumulation begins, not ends. Chart two, valuation. The MVRV ratio is about 1.1. Past tops printed way up here in euphoria. Translation, at these levels, you're buying near fair value, not at a blow-off top. That matters. Chart three, who's buying? Whale wallets just hit a 2026 high, and long-term holders flipped to net buyers. While retail panic sells the dip, the patient money is quietly scooping it up and moving it to cold storage. Chart four, demand. Spot Bitcoin ETFs have pulled in around $58 billion since launch. For the first time ever, big institutions have a regulated standing bid for Bitcoin. That floor didn't exist last cycle. And new supply, after the 2024 having, only about 450 coins are minted a day. A demand floor against a shrinking trickle of new coins. That's the core of the scarcity bet. Now, the honest part. I'm not delusional. Here's what could blow this thesis up. ETFs also had outflows in May. Inflation is sticky, the Fed is on hold, and tight money is a real headwind. The cycle could be late. That 200-week line can break. The market can stay irrational longer than you can. So, I don't go all in. I dollar cost average. Small, regular buys I can stomach if it drops more. And I hold my own keys. Not your keys, not your coins. Self-custody always. I stack and trade on Apex. 24/7, no KYC. Links in the description if you want it. So, that's the thesis. Fair value, smart money accumulating, a structural demand floor, and shrinking supply. None of this is a promise. Bitcoin could absolutely go lower. I just like the risk and reward right here. Buying when it's scary is hard. That's usually the point. Do your own research and size it so you can sleep. That's why I'm buying here. Not advice, just my reasoning. Thanks for watching.
